messages.content: You are a senior HVAC knowledge-base reviewer. Validate extracted knowledge objects from one equipment manual. Accept only useful, grounded, reviewable operational knowledge supported by the evidence. Reject marketing claims, UI-only navigation, isolated terminal labels, pure part names, duplicate/noisy items, generic prose with no operational value, or items whose evidence does not support the structured payload. Supported knowledge_type values: fault_code, parameter_spec, performance_spec, maintenance_procedure, application_guidance, operational_sequence, fault_diagnostic_rule, diagnostic_step, symptom. Reply with strict JSON only.

Manual filename: ashrae_211_energy_audit_best_practices.pdf
Equipment class: controller

Review every candidate below. Preserve candidate_id exactly.
Allowed category_if_not values: null, marketing, ui_behavior, terminal_label, part_name, duplicate, unsupported_type, weak_evidence, other.

{"candidates": [{"candidate_id": "cand_8e32df888b6bb7a0", "knowledge_type": "parameter_spec", "title": "Energy Use Intensity (EUI) Definition", "summary": "EUI is total energy consumed by the building in one year divided by total gross floor area.", "structured_payload": {"parameter_name": "EUI", "formula": "total energy consumed / total gross floor area", "units": "kBtu/sq ft or GJ/sq m", "title": "Energy Use Intensity (EUI) Definition", "summary": "EUI is total energy consumed by the building in one year divided by total gross floor area."}, "evidence": "Best Practices for Energy Audits Using
ASHRAE 211 -2018 and RETScreen
Session F3: Energy Auditing

ASHRAE 211 -2018 and RETScreen

Preliminary Energy
Use Analysis:
•Baseline overview of
building's energy
performance
•Early Stage Benchmarking
•Data Sources: utility bills,
schedules (min. 1 calendar
year)
•Key Metrics:
•EUI
•ECI
•Informs depth & focus of
main audit", "source_pages": [7, 8], "trust_level": "L4"}, {"candidate_id": "cand_814a5ea51bf4421d", "knowledge_type": "performance_spec", "title": "Model Calibration Tolerance", "summary": "Energy model calibration should be within ±10% of real energy consumption.", "structured_payload": {"tolerance": "±10%", "title": "Model Calibration Tolerance", "summary": "Energy model calibration should be within ±10% of real energy consumption."}, "evidence": "After the Audit
•Model Calibration
•Is the process that allows us to
compare the energy consumption
calculated by the model, with the
real energy consumption.
•Good practice: it should be in the
range of ±10%
•Schedules are important to know,
and be able to correctly put them
on the model
Let’s go with Live Example!
Some Resources
(particularly this one)", "source_pages": [15], "trust_level": "L3"}, {"candidate_id": "cand_47f18a162782cce1", "knowledge_type": "application_guidance", "title": "Audit Team Composition for Level 1", "summary": "Basic team for Level 1 audit includes energy auditor (solo) and optionally an energy analyst.", "structured_payload": {"level": "1", "roles": ["energy auditor", "energy analyst (optional)"], "title": "Audit Team Composition for Level 1", "summary": "Basic team for Level 1 audit includes energy auditor (solo) and optionally an energy analyst."}, "evidence": "Best Practices for Energy Audits Using
